sourceMovie.title: Sword of the Stranger
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sword of the Stranger (2007) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sword of the Stranger clicked for you, Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Kagemusha
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sword of the Stranger clicked for you, Kagemusha (1980) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball: Curse of the Blood Rubies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sword of the Stranger clicked for you, Dragon Ball: Curse of the Blood Rubies (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball: Sleeping Princess in Devil's Castle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sword of the Stranger clicked for you, Dragon Ball: Sleeping Princess in Devil's Castle (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sword of the Stranger?
faq.answer: Try Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon, Kagemusha, Dragon Ball: Curse of the Blood Rubies—each shares DNA with Sword of the Stranger while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sword of the Stranger?
faq.answer: Sword of the Stranger (2007) is best described as Action, Animation, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sword of the Stranger worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
